task_completion.description: Open Account Settings for Local Folders
task_completion.visual_evidence: Thunderbird Account Settings window is open, with 'Local Folders' (or its 'Copies & Folders' sub-menu) selected in the left navigation pane.
The 'Archive options' dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set archive granularity to a single folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Archive options dialog, the radio button for 'A single folder' is selected, ensuring no yearly or monthly subfolders will be created.

task_completion.description: Archive messages from the Local Folders Inbox
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Inbox under 'Local Folders' is shown as empty, or screenshots show all messages in this Inbox being selected and the 'Archive' action being triggered.

task_completion.description: Verify messages are moved to the Archives folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Archives' folder under 'Local Folders' is visible and contains the emails that were previously in the Inbox.

integrity.description: Agent used the Thunderbird GUI to configure settings and archive emails
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Thunderbird's menus, settings dialogs, and email list, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or text editors to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing 'mv' commands moving mail files or text editors modifying 'prefs.js' directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine provided email dataset
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails visible in the Thunderbird interface appear to be real emails (from the SpamAssassin corpus) and not manually typed placeholder messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target account for archiving is strictly 'Local Folders'. The archiving setting must be configured to 'A single folder' (which corresponds to the underlying preference archive_granularity=0). At least 90% of the original Inbox emails must be successfully preserved in the Archives folder.
